message:
fix(newproj): redirect gum stderr to /dev/tty so input renders (#269)

Wizard prompts (`acfs newproj --interactive`) accepted typed input
but the input field stayed blank while typing — pressing Enter still
submitted the value, but the user couldn't see what they were
typing. The wizard felt broken on first-run.

gum (Bubble Tea under the hood) draws its live TUI to stderr and
emits the final selected/input value on stdout. The wizard captured
stdout in shell variables (correct) but redirected stderr to
/dev/null, which discarded the live UI. Switching the redirect to
/dev/tty restores the visible UI without affecting the captured
value on stdout.

Applied to all 9 gum input / gum choose call sites in:

- scripts/lib/newproj_tui.sh (3)
- scripts/lib/newproj_screens/screen_directory.sh
- scripts/lib/newproj_screens/screen_project_name.sh
- scripts/lib/newproj_screens/screen_tech_stack.sh (2)
- scripts/lib/newproj_screens/screen_features.sh (2)

Reporter (jvaug) confirmed the equivalent local patch unblocks the
wizard. `bash -n` clean across all five files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -456,11 +456,17 @@ read_text_input() {
     while [[ "$valid" != "true" ]]; do
         if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" && "$TERM_HAS_COLOR" == "true" ]]; then
             # Use gum for pretty input
+            # Redirect gum's TUI stream to /dev/tty (not /dev/null) so
+            # keystrokes render visibly while we still capture the
+            # final value on stdout. Bubble Tea (gum's TUI engine)
+            # draws the live interface to stderr; sending stderr to
+            # /dev/null produces a working-but-invisible input field
+            # — see acfs#269.
             input=$(gum input \
                 --placeholder "${default:-Type here...}" \
                 --prompt "$prompt: " \
                 --prompt.foreground "#89b4fa" \
-                --cursor.foreground "#cba6f7" < /dev/tty 2>/dev/null) || true
+                --cursor.foreground "#cba6f7" < /dev/tty 2>/dev/tty) || true
         else
             # Fallback to read from /dev/tty to avoid stdin conflicts
             # from signal handlers or subshell capture (issue #153)
@@ -545,7 +551,7 @@ read_selection() {
             --cursor.foreground "#cba6f7" \
             --selected.foreground "#a6e3a1" \
             < /dev/tty \
-            "${options[@]}" 2>/dev/null) || true
+            "${options[@]}" 2>/dev/tty) || true
     else
         newproj_tty_printf "%s\n" "$prompt"
         local i=1
@@ -579,7 +585,7 @@ read_checkbox() {
             --cursor.foreground "#cba6f7" \
             --selected.foreground "#a6e3a1" \
             < /dev/tty \
-            "${options[@]}" 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ') || true
+            "${options[@]}" 2>/dev/tty | tr '\n' ' ') || true
     else
         newproj_tty_printf "%s\n" "$prompt (enter numbers separated by spaces, or 'all')"
         local i=1
